title: Source to Shore station container
summary: Give the three-beat Source to Shore beta its own compact station/board identity after High Pass, replacing the awkward completed-board launch-card fit without creating a larger planner.

execution_order.goal: Define the smallest Source to Shore station container that can house active, ready, and filed beta states cleanly.
execution_order.why_now: The old `EDGE LINE LOGGED` board is now carrying a second chapter and will not scale cleanly.

execution_order.goal: Implement the dedicated compact container while preserving existing Route v2 and Source to Shore state behavior.
execution_order.why_now: A clear home for the beta chapter is the main structural move that unlocks steadier future work.

execution_order.goal: Review the container for density, handheld fit, state continuity, and absence of planner or dashboard drift.
execution_order.why_now: This is the largest UX structure change in the push and should gate the downstream route-flow and payoff work.

completion_gate: After High Pass, Source to Shore is represented by a dedicated compact station/board identity rather than a launch card inside the completed first-season board.
Active, ready-to-file, and filed Source to Shore states remain reachable through existing Route v2 seams.
The existing first-season completed state still reads cleanly and is not broken by the beta container.
Focused station/board/runtime coverage passes, with browser proof if the visual shell changes materially.
guardrails: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No larger planner, dashboard, quest log, loadout, inventory, economy, combat, or direct API mode.
Keep the container station-first and compact.
Reuse existing Source to Shore and Route v2 state where possible.
Do not change save schema unless scout proves there is no behavior-preserving path.
post_packet_path: Use the new container as the anchor for route-flow consolidation.
Defer content payoff and spatial polish until the container is reviewed clean.

scout_handoff.surface_gate: Packet `168` has clean critic review in `docs/reports/2026-04-28-steady-beta-surface-triage-review.md`.
recommended_shape: Add a dedicated Source to Shore board identity, preferably `routeId: 'source-to-shore-beta'`, with `routeTitle: 'SOURCE TO SHORE'` and `branchLabel: 'Treeline -> Forest -> Coastal Scrub'.
Represent exactly three board beats: `source-shelter`, `forest-release`, and `dune-catch`.
Return `launchCard: null` for the Source to Shore route board so the routes page shows the beta beat rows instead of a launch card inside the completed edge board.
Drive active, ready-to-file, and filed states from `resolveSourceToShoreState(save)` and existing Route v2 progress.
Extend `getRouteBeatIdForRequest` for the three Source to Shore request ids so ready-to-file states keep notebook-ready behavior.
implementation_scope: Small renderer target if needed: `src/engine/field-station-routes-page.ts` for Source to Shore progress label handling.
Browser proof: active Source to Shore station board plus one ready-to-file or filed Source to Shore station board at native `256x160`.
non_goals: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No planner, dashboard, new station tab, save schema, route framework, loadout, inventory, economy, combat, or direct API mode.
Do not remove the first-season logged identity from the atlas or pre-Source completed edge states.
Do not change Source to Shore route behavior, journal behavior, map labels, support behavior, expedition-page card behavior, or filed memory notices except for route-board identity expectations.

runtime_changes: `src/engine/field-season-board.ts` now returns a dedicated `source-to-shore-beta` board with `SOURCE TO SHORE` title, three visible Source to Shore beats, no Source to Shore launch card, and ready-to-file notebook beat mapping.
`src/engine/field-station-routes-page.ts` keeps Source to Shore filed progress as `FILED` instead of generic `LOGGED`.
`src/engine/field-season-wrap.ts` preserves the first-season archive strip while the Source to Shore board is active.
`src/engine/field-station-homecoming-shell.ts` keeps the late-season archive lintel for Source to Shore archive states without relying on a launch card.
`src/engine/nursery.ts` preserves the existing salmonberry capstone hint while Source to Shore is active.
proof: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t 'Source to Shore|journal|debug save snapshots'`
Native `256x160` browser proof at `output/lane-1-main-450-browser/` with empty `errors.json`.
critic_focus: Confirm the board is compact and readable at `256x160` across active, ready-to-file, and filed states.
Confirm no fourth beat, save schema, planner, dashboard, route framework, or support-system drift landed.
Confirm first-season archive strip, atlas logged-route history, late-season station lintel, and nursery capstone hint remain intact.

confirmed: The station now uses a dedicated `source-to-shore-beta` board with `SOURCE TO SHORE`, exactly three Source to Shore beats, and `launchCard: null`.
Active, ready-to-file, and filed states remain driven by `resolveSourceToShoreState(save)` and existing Route v2 progress.
The first-season archive strip, atlas logged-route history, late-season station lintel, and nursery capstone hint remain intact.
No fourth beat, planner, dashboard, save schema, route framework, or support-system drift landed.
Native `256x160` active, ready-to-file, and filed browser proof is readable with empty browser errors.
proof: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t 'Source to Shore|journal|debug save snapshots'`
